name: Omega Chess
author: Daniel MacDonald (1992) / AGP port
description: Omega Chess (Daniel MacDonald, 1992): the commercial 104-square variant endorsed by grandmasters Michael Rohde and Alex Sherzer. A 10x10 board plus four extra 'wizard' squares attached diagonally beyond each corner. The standard army is joined by two Champions (step 1 orthogonally, leap 2 orthogonally or diagonally) on the corner squares and two Wizards (step 1 diagonally, (1,3) camel leap; colour-bound) on the wizard squares. Pawns may advance 1, 2 or 3 squares on their first move and can be captured en passant on any passed square; castling is orthodox in form (king two squares toward either rook); pawns promote on the far rank of the 10x10 field to any piece except a king. Checkmate wins.
